data.title: Arrowhead unit sells rights for plozasiran to Sanofi in Greater China
data.description:
Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ARWR) said it has signed an asset purchase agreement between Sanofi and a subsidiary of the company, Visirna Therapeutics, which focuses on developing cardiometabolic treatments in Greater China.

Under the agreement, Sanofi will obtain an exclusive license to further develop and commercialize plozasiran in Greater China, which aims to provide a new treatment option for individuals with high triglyceride levels.

Sanofi will make an initial payment of $130M and could pay up to an additional $265M based on regulatory achievements related to the investigational drug plozasiran, which Visirna Therapeutics has the rights to in this region.

Additionally, Arrowhead stands to earn royalties from the sales of this product in Greater China.

data.title: Fluor plunges after wide Q2 miss, steep full-year guidance cut
data.description:
Fluor (NYSE:FLR) -20.8% pre-market Friday, posied to open at a nearly two-month low, after missing estimates for Q2 adjusted earnings and revenues, and cutting full-year financial guidance.

Fluor (NYSE:FLR) issued downside guidance for FY 2025, now seeing adjusted earnings of of $1.95-$2.15/share, down from prior guidance of $2.25-$2.75 and well below the $2.58 analyst consensus estimate from FactSet, and forecast full-year adjusted EBITDA of $475M-$525M, compared to its previous outlook for $575M-$675M, citing "client hesitation around economic uncertainty and its impact on new awards and project delays and results for the quarter."

Q2 results were hurt by "three long-standing infrastructure projects and a shift in expected capital spending from some clients," CEO Jim Breuer said. "We view this shift as temporary and believe that our long-term strategy centered around disciplined project delivery in growth markets will continue to benefit our clients and our shareholders."

data.title: Xcel Brands announces pricing of $2.6M public offering and concurrent management-led private placement
data.description:
• Xcel Brands (NASDAQ:XELB) on Friday announced the pricing of its public offering of 2.18M shares of common stock at a public offering price of $1.10 per share.
  • In a concurrent private placement, the company also agreed to issue and sell an aggregate of 145,147 unregistered shares to certain insiders of the company, including company’s CEO, Robert D'Loren, at a purchase price of $1.36, which is equal to the closing price of the company’s common stock on July 31, 2025.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about August 4.
  • The combined gross proceeds from the public offering and concurrent private placement are expected to be approximately $2.6 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for brand development and launch, working capital, and other general corporate purposes, including payment of outstanding payables.
  • XELB -19.11% premarket to $1.1.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nexalin Technology appoints Justin Van Fleet as CFO
data.description:
• Nexalin Technology (NASDAQ:NXL) announced on Friday the appointment of Justin Van Fleet as Chief Financial Officer, effective August 1, 2025.
  • Van Fleet brings more than 20 years of experience in public accounting and assurance services, most recently serving as Partner at Marcum LLP.

data.title: Oxford Square Capital prices public offering of $65 million
data.description:
• Oxford Square Capital (NASDAQ:OXSQ) announced on Friday that it has priced an underwritten public offering of $65 million in aggregate principal amount of 7.75% unsecured notes due 2030.
  • The notes will mature on July 31, 2030, and may be redeemed in whole or in part at any time or from time to time at the company’s option on or after July 31, 2027.
  • The notes will bear interest at a rate of 7.75% per year payable quarterly on January 31, April 30, July 31, and October 31 of each year, commencing October 31, 2025.
  • The offering is expected to close on August 7, 2025.
  • The company has granted the underwriters an option to purchase up to an additional $9.75 million in aggregate principal amount of notes.
  •  The notes are expected to be listed on the NASDAQ Global Select Market and to trade thereon within 30 days of the original issue date under the trading symbol “OXSQH”.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to repay indebtedness, acquire investments in accordance with our investment objective and strategies described in this prospectus supplement and for general corporate purposes.
  • OXSQ -1.33% premarket to $2.299.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jobs data in charts: Revisions lead to plunge in 3-month average of payroll growth
Performant Healthcare announces preliminary Q2 results
data.description:
• Performant Healthcare (NASDAQ:PHLT) announced on Friday preliminary financial results for the second quarter of 2025.
  • The company anticipates reporting second quarter revenues of approximately $37.8M, net income of approximately $2.1M, and adjusted EBITDA of approximately $6.2M.
  • In a separate press release, Performant announced its entry into a definitive agreement to be acquired by Machinify, a healthcare intelligence company. 
  • Performant is also withdrawing its previously issued full-year 2025 guidance.

data.title: Brookfield Renewable declares $0.373 dividend
data.description:
• Brookfield Renewable  (NYSE:BEPC) declares $0.373/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.07%
  • Payable Sept. 30; for shareholders of record Aug. 29; ex-div Aug. 29.
  • The company has now announced a dividend of $0.373 for three consecutive quarters.
  • See BEPC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Pfizer/BioNTech lose appeal to reverse Moderna’s COVID vaccine patent win in U.K.
data.description:
London's Court of Appeal on Friday struck down a case filed by Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX), in a bid to reverse a prior High Court ruling that granted a patent win to their rival Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA).

The makers of the Comirnaty COVID shot filed legal submissions last month to overturn the July 2024 ruling that determined one of Moderna’s (NASDAQ:MRNA) patents related to its Spikevax COVID-19 vaccine was valid.

At the time, Judge Richard Meade of London's High Court found that Comirnaty had infringed Moderna’s (NASDAQ:MRNA) EP’949 patent, meaning Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) were liable to pay damages to the Massachusetts-area biotech based on their vaccine sales after 2022.

However, the duo was permitted to appeal the ruling, which was upheld by Judge Richard Arnold of London's Court of Appeal on Friday.

The ruling "does not change our unwavering stance that this patent is invalid," Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) said, adding that they plan to appeal, Reuters reported, quoting a joint statement from the company. "This decision has no immediate impact on Pfizer and BioNTech or Comirnaty.”

The case marks one of many COVID vaccine-related patent disputes the two parties are engaged in across multiple countries, including the U.S.

data.title: Canada Goose breaks losing streak with Wells Fargo upgrade
data.description:
After retreating for six consecutive days off a 2-year-high, shares of Canada Goose (NYSE:GOOS) are set to open in the green thanks to an upgrade at Wells Fargo.

Analyst Ike Boruchow moved to the bullish camp with an Overweight rating on the outerwear, apparel, and footwear manufacturer, as “material improvements in product and marketing are driving meaningful [direct-to-consumer] growth, while wholesale upside now appears more visible.”

The turnaround has been more than 24 months in the making with production shifts, higher ad spending and a 30% cut to unproductive lines, setting up Canada Goose (NYSE:GOOS) for success for the first time in several years. And while yesterday’s fiscal first quarter earnings report “may have had some holes,” there was enough compelling key performance indicators within the report to suggest that investments in product and marketing have led to an inflection point.

Furthermore, the 14% slide in the stock on Thursday in the wake of its earnings report represents an attractive entry point, Burochow says, especially given double-digit DTC comparable sales.

For the fiscal first quarter ended June 29, Canada Goose (NYSE:GOOS) saw a 22.4% increase in total sales, with DTC sales up 23.8% and wholesale sales climbing 12%. Additionally, due to a higher number of Friends & Family events, “Other Revenue” increased more than 30%, all of which drove gross profit up 25% to C$66.2M and the company’s gross margin to 61.4%, a 170 basis points improvement from the same quarter last year.

However, due to a one-time financial settlement of C$43.8M and costs associated with expanding its global retail network, increased marketing spend for new ad campaigns, and investments in design and merchandising, the company reported a wider than expected loss of C$0.91 per share versus C$0.79 per share a year ago.

But despite the wider loss, Burochow sees the stock as “too cheap for the story that is developing,” specifically with a 6x out-year EBITDA.

“While we acknowledge GOOS still needs to invest (and likely keep margins somewhat under pressure in the near term) we still see out-year EPS power of ~C$1.50 should top-line strategies sustain.”  

After Thursday’s double-digit loss in the share price, Canada Goose (GOOS) is set to open more than 3% higher, snapping a 6-day losing streak.
